example.title: ZIO HTTP Server
example.description: Example of a simple ZIO HTTP server implementing a REST API endpoint.
example.code:
import zio._
import zio.http._
import zio.json._

case class User(id: String, name: String, email: String)
object User:
  given JsonEncoder[User] = DeriveJsonEncoder.gen[User]
  given JsonDecoder[User] = DeriveJsonDecoder.gen[User]

object UserService:
  val routes: Routes[Any, Nothing] =
    Routes(
      Method.GET / "users" -> handler {
        val users = List(
          User("1", "Alice", "alice@example.com"),
          User("2", "Bob", "bob@example.com")
        )
        Response.json(users.toJsonPretty)
      },

      Method.GET / "users" / string("id") -> handler { (id: String, _: Request) =>
        val user = User(id, s"User $id", s"user$id@example.com")
        Response.json(user.toJsonPretty)
      },

      Method.POST / "users" -> handler { (req: Request) =>
        for
          body <- req.body.asString
          user <- ZIO.fromEither(body.fromJson[User]).mapError(e => Response.badRequest(e))
        yield Response.json(user.toJsonPretty).status(Status.Created)
      }
    )

object Main extends ZIOAppDefault:
  def run = Server.serve(UserService.routes).provide(Server.default)
notes: ZIO HTTP uses direct-style Scala 3 syntax with ZIO effects
All routes are purely functional and composable
